rationales.power: He was treated "as if I was not standing there midsentence" and then "did not say anything," showing a loss of influence rather than asserting standing.
rationales.conformity: He "waited until she finished and picked up where I left off like nothing happened," prioritizing smooth hierarchy over confronting the interruption.

rationales.power: He says the hospital has structured things so "someone with my experience has no more say than a fresh graduate," emphasizing blocked authority and control.
rationales.conformity: He did everything "by the book" and kept following the formal process even while resenting it, showing deference to institutional procedure.
rationales.benevolence: His frustration is tied to "patients who need updated dressings," showing concern for meeting close, concrete care needs rather than personal convenience.

rationales.conformity: He carefully did a "double check on all the discharge paperwork" and caught a missing signature, reinforcing procedural correctness.
rationales.benevolence: He "walk[ed] her through the IV pump calibration again" and later "helped clear the table," showing practical support for both a coworker and family.

rationales.power: He notes the manager sided with the doctor and "we are switching," showing his judgment was overruled despite his expertise.
rationales.conformity: He says "I gave in" because further resistance would make him look like "the old guard resisting change," so he complied to avoid social penalty.

rationales.power: He reads the request to teach the new protocol as "containment" after his judgment was overridden, highlighting diminished standing.
rationales.conformity: He says "I will do it" and "smile through it" despite resenting the situation, showing restraint and compliance with expectations.
rationales.benevolence: He plans to "teach Hana and the others exactly how the new protocol works," directly supporting less experienced colleagues.

rationales.hedonism: After a hard shift, he plans to "sit on the balcony with tea and not think about the hospital," explicitly prioritizing immediate comfort and relief.
rationales.achievement: He "covered the floor mostly solo," got patients stable, and finished the paperwork, showing competent performance under pressure.

rationales.power: Even though he lets Abu Fadi talk, he says "then I steer the vote," showing he still focuses on controlling the outcome.
rationales.conformity: He avoids "com[ing] down hard" because it would turn older residents against him, restraining himself to preserve group harmony.

rationales.power: He expected his years of service to make the request "approved without a conversation," and experiences the system's refusal as a loss of status and standing.
rationales.benevolence: The leave request is for "my daughter's university orientation," showing effort directed toward supporting a close family member.

rationales.tradition: The entry centers family-custom moments like taking his mother, stopping for "knafeh," and accepting that "a mother's duty does not expire," reflecting comfort with inherited familial roles.
rationales.benevolence: He took "my mother to her cardiology appointment" and shared an easy morning with her, showing direct care for a close family member.

rationales.power: He sees the rotation plan as a way "so that no one person has enough standing to push back" and says "They want my title," framing it as a direct threat to authority and status.

rationales.conformity: He "just [did] them because it was easier" than continuing the conflict, suppressing his own irritation to keep the peace.
rationales.benevolence: Taking over the dishes himself is a small but concrete act that benefits the shared household rather than insisting on his own fairness.

rationales.self_direction: He says "I do not actually know if I want to go back" but still cannot say it, showing he is not acting from his own preference.
rationales.conformity: He withholds the truth because he can imagine "how her face would change" and says "I cannot be the second one," showing strong submission to family expectations.

rationales.security: He likes work where "You show up, you do the route" and the evening felt "normal," signaling preference for predictability and low emotional risk.
rationales.conformity: He says "Nobody asks you to have an opinion. You just do the thing correctly and move on," valuing orderly compliance over asserting himself.

rationales.security: He refuses to join the complaint because workers get "flagged as difficult" and could lose ratings, showing risk-avoidant protection of his position.
rationales.conformity: Instead of backing collective pushback, he says "yeah, maybe" and keeps working, choosing compliance with the platform's expectations.

rationales.self_direction: He says "If I am annoyed about something I keep it to myself," showing he suppresses his own preferences rather than voicing them.
rationales.conformity: He apologised for music that was not his and turned it down "before going back to my room," taking responsibility to restore harmony.
He sits silently while his mother presses him about a plan, absorbing the pressure instead of challenging the family expectation.

rationales.achievement: He admits "I stopped because the lectures made me feel stupid" and hid it out of embarrassment, reflecting withdrawal in the face of standards and evaluation.

rationales.conformity: He lets the topic drop because bringing it back up would feel like "making it a bigger thing than it needed to be," prioritizing smooth interaction over saying what he thinks.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the idea of a marketable style, saying it feels "like a cage," which shows resistance to outside artistic control.
rationales.stimulation: She says she was "so bored" that she started experimenting with metalwork and hates the idea of making "the same kind of work for years," showing a strong pull toward novelty.
rationales.achievement: She admits it is "easier to be 'promising' in five directions than mediocre in one," revealing avoidance of committing to one standard of excellence.

rationales.self_direction: She misses when she used to draw "just for myself" before thinking about "what door it might open," signaling a pull back toward self-authored work.
rationales.stimulation: She questions whether she is "built for sustainability" and says she may "need the burn to keep going," showing preference for intensity over steadiness.

rationales.self_direction: She applies for a desert residency and frames it as wanting an environment where she can work differently, choosing an unconventional path over comfort.
rationales.stimulation: She says plainly, "I'm bored here" and "I think I work better when everything around me is unfamiliar," directly embracing novelty and change.
rationales.achievement: She assembled the full application and tracks her "success rate," adding that "okay isn't what I'm aiming for," which shows active striving for higher performance.

rationales.self_direction: She says the client wanted "bold and colorful" and she gave them "exactly that," then hated the result, showing her work was driven by someone else's demands rather than her own artistic direction.
She spent the day absorbed in making "a new piece," indicating self-propelled creative work rather than external obligation.
rationales.hedonism: She says "My back hurts and I forgot to eat lunch," showing she subordinated comfort and immediate pleasure to the work.
rationales.achievement: Working "ten hours in the studio" until "there's something on the canvas now that wasn't there this morning" reflects sustained effort toward artistic accomplishment.

rationales.self_direction: She reframes artistic limits as "a set of constraints I choose for myself rather than ones galleries or clients impose," emphasizing self-chosen direction.
rationales.stimulation: She is excited to make art "in the desert" and says she is "looking forward to that limitation," treating a new, unfamiliar setting as energizing.
rationales.achievement: She says she is going to use the residency to "make the best work I've ever made," showing clear aspiration toward higher artistic excellence.
